title: Portfolio Benchmark Analytics — Bloomberg PORT-style tracking error, IR, active share, capture ratios

observation.gap_identified: Bloomberg PORT's core benchmark-relative analytics (tracking error, information ratio, active share, up/down capture ratios) were completely missing. These are the #1 institutional portfolio analytics that separate professional tools from amateur ones.

implementation.feature_built: Full benchmark analytics service with 9 analytics: (1) Annualized tracking error, (2) Information ratio (Grinold & Kahn), (3) Active share with Cremers & Petajisto 2009 classification, (4) Up/down capture ratios (monthly geometric), (5) Rolling tracking error with trend detection, (6) OLS regression (beta, alpha, R², residual vol) vs benchmark, (7) Multi-period return comparison (1m/3m/6m/1y/YTD), (8) Side-by-side risk comparison (Sharpe/Sortino/max DD), (9) Interpretation engine with management style classification (Stock Picker / Factor Bet / Closet Indexer). Wired into existing POST /api/portfolio/analyze (summary) + new standalone POST /api/portfolio/benchmark (full detail). Frontend TypeScript types and API client function added.

limitations: Active share uses approximate market-cap-weighted benchmark weights (exact SPY constituent weights require premium data feed)
No network integration tests — all 29 tests use synthetic data to avoid flaky yfinance calls
Rolling tracking error time series only covers last 52 weeks
assessment.self_critique: Could have added a Morningstar-style style box classification (3x3 value/blend/growth x small/mid/large) as a companion feature. The active share approximation is rough but serviceable for a free tool — would be much better with real index constituent weights from a data provider.

next_steps: Add Morningstar-style style box analysis using factor model data we already have
Wire benchmark_analytics into AI portfolio commentary (LLM can reference tracking error, IR, management style)
Add income/dividend projection for portfolio (Morningstar feature gap)
Consider adding custom benchmark support (user picks any ETF as benchmark)
